questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Suppose $f$ and $g$ are differentiable functions such that $x g(f(x)) f^{\prime}(g(x)) g^{\prime}(x)$ $=f(g(x)) g^{\prime}(f(x)) f^{\prime}(x)$ for all real $x$. Also $f$ is non negative and $g$ is positive. If $\int_0^a f(g(x)) d x=\frac{1}{2}-\frac{e^{-2 a}}{2}$ for all reals $a$ and $g(f(0))=1$ then the value of $g(f(4))$ is equal to $e^{-\lambda}$ where $\lambda \in N$, find the value of $\lambda$.
questions.solution:
$$
\begin{aligned}
\frac{x f^{\prime}(g(x)) g^{\prime}(x)}{f(g(x))} & =\frac{g^{\prime}(f(x)) f^{\prime}(x)}{g(f(x))} \\
x \frac{d}{d x}(\ln f(g(x))) & =\frac{d}{d x}(\ln g(f(x)))...(1) \\ Also \int_0^a f(g(x)) d x & =\frac{1}{2}-\frac{e^{-2 a}}{2} \forall a
\end{aligned}
$$
$\therefore$ On differentiate, we get $f(g(a))=e^{-2 a} \Rightarrow f(g(x))=e^{-2 x}...(2)$
From Eqns. (1) and (2), we get
$$
\begin{aligned}
x \frac{d}{d x}\left(\ln e^{-2 x}\right) & =\frac{d}{d x}(\ln g(f(x))) \\
-2 x & =\frac{d}{d x}(\ln g(f(x)))
\end{aligned}
$$
On integrating, we get
$$
\begin{array}{ll} 
& \ln g(f(x))=-x^2+C \\
& g(f(x))=k \cdot e^{-x^2} \\
\text { Given } & g(f(0))=1 \quad \Rightarrow k=1 \\
\therefore \quad & g(f(4))=e^{-16} \Rightarrow \lambda=16
\end{array}
$$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Let the solution of the equation $\frac{d y}{d x}=y+\int_0^2 y d x$ is $y(x)$. If $y(0)=1$, then find the absolute value of $[y(2)]$.
[Note: $[k]$ denotes greatest integer function less than or equal to $k$.]
questions.solution:
Let
$$
\begin{aligned}
A & =\int_0^2 y d x \\
\frac{d y}{y+A} & =d x
\end{aligned}
$$

Its solution is
$$
\ln (y+A)=x+c
$$

Given
$$
\begin{aligned}
y(0) & =1, \text { so } c=\ln (1+A) \\
Y & =(1+A) e^x-A \\
A & =\int_0^2 y d x=\int_0^2\left((1+A) e^x-A\right) d x=\frac{e^2-1}{4-e^2}
\end{aligned}
$$

Hence solution is
$$
y(x)=\frac{3 e^x-e^2+1}{4-e^2}
$$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $f: R-\{1\} \rightarrow R-\{1\}$ be a function satisfying the differential equation $2 x(y+x) d x-x^2(d x+d y)=(x+y)^2 d x$ with $f(2)=2$. If area enclosed by $y=f(x)$ and $x$-axis from $x=2$ to $x=3$ is $(a+\ln b)$ where $a, b \in N$, then find the value of $(a+b)$.
questions.solution:
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \quad x^2 d y+y^2 d x=0 \\
& \Rightarrow \quad \frac{d y}{y^2}+\frac{d x}{x^2}=0 \Rightarrow \frac{-1}{y}-\frac{1}{x}=C \\
& (2,2) \Rightarrow C=1 \\
& \therefore \quad \frac{1}{y}+\frac{1}{x}=1 \Rightarrow y=\frac{x}{x-1} \\
& \text { Required area }=\int_2^3 \frac{x}{x-1} d x=\int_2^3\left(1+\frac{1}{x-1}\right) d x \Rightarrow(x+\ln (x-1))_2^3=1+\ln 2 \equiv a+\ln b
\end{aligned}
$$
Hence, $a+b=3$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If solution of equation $\frac{d y}{d x}=y+\int_0^2 y d x$, is $y(x)$. Then $[\mid y(2) \|$ is equal to $\qquad$ (where [.] greatest integer function) $($ given $y(0)=1)$
questions.solution:
$\frac{d y}{d x}=y+\int_0^2 y d x$

Say $\int_0^2 y d x=A \Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=y+A \Rightarrow \frac{d y}{y+A}=d x \Rightarrow \ln (y+A)=x+B$
Given $y(0)=1 \Rightarrow B=\ln (1+A) \Rightarrow \ln \frac{(y+A)}{(1+A)}=x \Rightarrow y+A=(1+A) e^x$
Now find ' $A$ '
$A=\int_0^2\left[(1+A) e^x-A \left\lvert\, d x \Rightarrow A=\frac{e^2-1}{4-e^2}\right.\right.$

Hence solution $\mathrm{y}(\mathrm{x})=\left(1+\frac{\mathrm{e}^2-1}{4-\mathrm{e}^2}\right) \mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x}}-\left(\frac{\mathrm{e}^2-1}{4-\mathrm{e}^2}\right)=\frac{3 \mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x}}-\mathrm{e}^2+1}{4-\mathrm{e}^2}$
$\begin{aligned}
& y(2)=\frac{2 e^2+1}{4-e^2} \\
& \Rightarrow\|y(2)\|=4
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $y=y(x)$ satisfy the differential equation $\left(2 x y+x^2 y+\frac{y^3}{3}\right) d x+\left(x^2+y^2\right) d y=0$. If $y(1)=1$ and the value of $(y(0))^3=k e(k \in N)$. Find $k$.
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} &\left(2 x y d x+x^2 d y\right)+x^2 y d x+\left(\frac{y^3}{3} d x+y^2 d y\right)=0 \\ & x^2 y=t \text { and } \frac{y^3}{3}=u \\ &(d t+t d x)+(u d x+d u)=0 \\ & d t+d u=-(t+u) d x \\ & \int \frac{d t+d u}{t+u}=-\int d x \\ & \ln (t+u)=-x+C \Rightarrow x^2 y+\frac{y^3}{3}=k^{\prime} e^{-x} \text { at } x=1, y=1 \\ & \frac{4}{3}=\frac{k^{\prime}}{e} \Rightarrow k^{\prime}=\frac{4 e}{3}\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} x^2 y+\frac{y^3}{3} & =\frac{4 e}{3} ; \text { put } x=0 \\ \frac{y^3}{3} & =\frac{4 e}{3} \\ \therefore \quad y^3(0) & =4 e \Rightarrow k=4\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
For the differential equation $\frac{d y}{d x}+i y=2 \sin (x)$, with initial condition $y(0)=\frac{3}{2}$. If $y(\pi)=\frac{a}{b}+c \pi i$, for co-prime integers $a$ and $b$, find $a b c$.
[Note: $i=\sqrt{-1}$ denotes the imaginary unit.]
questions.solution:
$$
\frac{d y}{d x}+i y=2 \sin (x)
$$
(Linear D.E. with integrating factor $e^{i x}$ )

Hence,
$$
\begin{aligned}
y e^{i x}=\int 2 \sin x e^{i x} d x & =\int 2 \sin x(\cos x+i \sin x) d x \\
& =\sin ^2 x+i\left(x-\frac{1}{2} \sin 2 x\right)+c
\end{aligned}
$$

Given
$$
\begin{aligned}
y(0) & =\frac{3}{2} \Rightarrow c=\frac{3}{2} \\
\therefore & y e^{i x}=\sin ^2 x+i\left(x-\frac{1}{2} \sin 2 x\right)+\frac{3}{2}
\end{aligned}
$$

Now put $x=\pi$
$$
\begin{aligned}
\therefore \quad y(\pi) e^{i \pi} & =0+i(\pi-0)+\frac{3}{2} \\
y(\pi)(-1) & =i \pi+\frac{3}{2}
\end{aligned}
$$

Hence,
$$
y(\pi)=-\frac{3}{2}-\pi i
$$

Therefore $\quad a b c=6$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Given $y(0)=2000$ and $\frac{d y}{d x}=32000-20 y^2$, then find the value of $\operatorname{Lim}_{x \rightarrow \infty} y(x)$.
questions.solution:
We have $\frac{d y}{d x}=20\left(1600-y^2\right)$
$\begin{aligned}
& \Rightarrow \int \frac{\mathrm{dy}}{(40)^2-\mathrm{y}^2}=20 \int \mathrm{dx} \\
& \Rightarrow \frac{1}{80} \ln \frac{40+y}{40-y}=20 x+C^{\prime} \text { or } \ln \frac{40+y}{40-y}=1600 x+C \\
& \Rightarrow \frac{40+\mathrm{y}}{40-\mathrm{y}}=\frac{\mathrm{ke}^{1600 \mathrm{x}}}{1} \text {, where } \mathrm{k}=\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{C}} \text { (let) } \\
& \Rightarrow \frac{2 \mathrm{y}}{80}=\frac{\mathrm{ke}^{1600 \mathrm{x}}-1}{\mathrm{ke}^{1600 \mathrm{x}}+1} \text { (using componendo \& dividendo) } \\
& \therefore \operatorname{Lim}_{\mathrm{x} \rightarrow \infty} \mathrm{y}=40 \operatorname{Lim}_{\mathrm{x} \rightarrow \infty}\left[\frac{\mathrm{k}-\mathrm{e}^{-1600 \mathrm{x}}}{\mathrm{k}-\mathrm{e}^{-1600 \mathrm{x}}}\right]=40
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The number of solutions of $x+\int_0^x \operatorname{In} t d t=\frac{x^2}{3},\left(x \in \mathrm{R}^{+}\right)$is __________ .
questions.solution:
$t \ln t-\left.t\right|_0 ^x+x=\frac{x^2}{3} \Rightarrow \ln x=\frac{x}{3}$
Now equation of tangent of the slope $\frac{1}{3}$ to the curve $y=\ln x$ at $(3, \ln 3)$ is
$y-\ln 3=\frac{1}{3}(x-3) \Rightarrow y=\frac{1}{3} x+(\ln 3-1)$
As, $\ln 3-1\gt0$, this tangent is above the line $y=\frac{x}{3}$.
So the line $\mathrm{y}=\frac{\mathrm{x}}{3}$ will cut $\mathrm{y}=\ln \mathrm{x}$ at two distinct points.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Let $C$ be a curve passing through $M(2,2)$ such that the slope of the tangent at any point to the curve is reciprocal of the ordinate of the point. If the area bounded by curve $C$ and line $x=2$ is expressed as a rational
$\frac{p}{q} \text { (where } p \text { and } q \text { are in their lowest form), then find }(p+q) \text {. }$
questions.solution:
Let $P(x, y)$ be any point on the curve $C$. Now, $\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}=\frac{1}{\mathrm{y}}$
Since the curve passes through $M(2,2)$, so $k=0$ $\Rightarrow y^2=2 x$
Hence required area $=2 \int_0^2 \sqrt{2 \mathrm{x}} \mathrm{dx}=2 \sqrt{2} \times \frac{2}{3}\left(\mathrm{x}^{3 / 2}\right)_0^2=\frac{4}{3} \sqrt{2} \times 2 \sqrt{2}=\frac{16}{3}$ (square unit) $\Rightarrow p+q=19$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $f(x)$ be a differentiable function satisfying $f(y)-f(x)=\frac{x^x}{y^y} f\left(\frac{y^y}{x^x}\right)$ for all $x, y \in R^{+}$. If $f^{\prime}(1)=1$, then find the value of $\left|f(e) f\left(\frac{1}{e}\right)\right|$.
questions.solution:
$$
[f(y)-f(x)] y^y=x^x f\left(\frac{y^y}{x^x}\right)
$$

Differentiate w.r.t. $x$ keeping $y$ constant.
$$
-f^{\prime}(x) \cdot y^y=x^x f\left(\frac{y^y}{x^x}\right)(1+\ln x)-x^x f^{\prime}\left(\frac{y^y}{x^x}\right) \cdot \frac{y^y(1+\ln x)}{x^x}
$$

Given $\quad f^{\prime}(1)=1$
Put
$$
y^y=x^x
$$
$$
-f^{\prime}(x) \cdot x^x=x^x f(1)(1+\ln x)-x^x f^{\prime}(1)(1+\ln x)
$$

Now, $\quad f(1)=0$
Hence, $\quad f^{\prime}(x)=1+\ln x$
Integrate
$$
\begin{aligned}
& f(x)=C+x \ln x \\
& f(1)=0 \Rightarrow C=0 \\
& f(x)=x \ln x
\end{aligned}
$$

So, $f(e)=e$ and $f(1 / e)=-1 / e$. So the answer is 1 .
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $F$ be the set of all continuous real valued functions which are solutions to $f^2(x)=100+\int_0^x\left(f(t) f^{\prime}(t)-f(t)-f^{\prime}(t)-1\right) d t$. Find the value of $\frac{1}{|F|} \sum_{f(x) \in F}|f(100)|$.
questions.solution:
$f(0)= \pm 10$
Differentiating both sides, we get
$$
\begin{array}{ll} 
& \underbrace{(f(x)+1)}_{\text {rejected }}\left(f^{\prime}(x)+1\right)=0 \\
\therefore & f(x)=10-x \text { or }-10-x \\
\text { Hence, } \quad & \frac{1}{|\mathrm{~F}|} \sum_{f(x) \in \mathrm{F}}|f(100)|=\frac{1}{2}(90+110)=100
\end{array}
$$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $f(1)+g(1)=9 e ; f(x)=-x^2 g^{\prime}(x) ; g(x)=-x^2 f^{\prime}(x)$. If $\int_1^4 \frac{f^{\prime}(x)+g(x)}{x^2} d x=k\left(e-e^{\frac{1}{4}}\right)$, then find the value of $k$.
questions.solution:
$$
\begin{aligned}
& f(1)+g(1)=9 e, f(x)=-x^2 g^{\prime}(x) \text { and } g(x)=-x^2 f^{\prime}(x) \\
& I=\int_1^4 \frac{f(x)+g(x)}{x^2} d x=\int_1^4 \frac{-x^2\left(f^{\prime}(x)+g^{\prime}(x)\right)}{x^2} d x=\int_1^4-\left(f^{\prime}(x)+g^{\prime}(x)\right) d x \\
& =-[f(x)+g(x)]_1^4=-(f(4)+g(4)-9 e)
\end{aligned}
$$

Now, $f(x)+g(x)=-x^2\left(g^{\prime}(x)+f^{\prime}(x)\right)$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \Rightarrow \frac{g^{\prime}(x)+f^{\prime}(x)}{f(x)+g(x)}=\frac{-1}{x^2} \Rightarrow f(x)+g(x)=a e^{\frac{1}{x}} \Rightarrow f^{\prime}(1)+g(1)=9 e \Rightarrow a=9 \\
& \Rightarrow f(x)+g(x)=9 e^{\frac{1}{x}} \\
& I=-\left(9 e^{\frac{1}{4}}-9 e\right)=9\left(e-e^{\frac{1}{4}}\right)=k\left(e-e^{\frac{1}{4}}\right) \Rightarrow k=9
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Consider a curve passing through $(1,1)$ such that perpendicular distance of normal drawn at any point $P$ on the curve from origin is equal to ordinate of the point $P$. If area enclosed by the curve is $A$, then find $[2 A]$.
[Note: $[k]$ denotes greatest integer less than or equal to $k$.]
questions.solution: Equation of normal at $P(x, y)$ $\begin{aligned} Y-y & =\frac{-1}{m}(X-x) \\ m Y-m y & =-X+x \\ x+m y-(x+m y) & =0 \\ \left|\frac{m y+x}{\sqrt{1+m^2}}\right| & =y\end{aligned}$ $\Rightarrow \quad m^2 y^2+x^2+2 x m y=m^2 y^2+y^2 \Rightarrow m=\frac{y^2-x^2}{2 x y}$ $\begin{gathered}2 x y \frac{d y}{d x}=y^2-x^2 \\ y^2=t \Rightarrow 2 y \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d t}{d x} \Rightarrow x \frac{d t}{d x}=t-x^2 \\ \Rightarrow \frac{d t}{d x}-\frac{t}{x}=-x \text { which is linear differential equation }\end{gathered}$ $\begin{aligned} & \text { I.F. }=e^{-\int \frac{d x}{x}}=\frac{1}{x} \\ & \therefore \quad t\left(\frac{1}{x}\right)=-\int 1 \cdot d x+C \Rightarrow \frac{y^2}{x}=-x+C \\ & \end{aligned}$ $(1,1) \Rightarrow C=-2$ $y^2=-x^2+2 x \Rightarrow x^2+y^2-2 x=0$ $$ \Rightarrow \quad(x-1)^2+y^2=1 $$ $\therefore$ Area bounded by the curve, $A=\pi$ $$ \therefore \quad[2 A]=[2 \pi]=6 $$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $f(x)$ and $g(x)$ be two function such that $f^{\prime \prime}(x)=2 f^{\prime}(x)-2 f(x), g(x)=f^{\prime}(x)-f(x)$. Let $F(x)$ be defined as $F(x)=\left[f^2(x)+g^2(x)\right]\left[f^2(-x)+g^2(-x)\right]$. If $F(0)=1$ then the value of $F(1)$ is $\qquad$
questions.solution:
$g(x)=f^{\prime}(x)-f(x)$....(1)
$g^{\prime}(x)=f^{\prime}(x)-2 f(x)$....(2)
$F^{\prime}(x)=\left(2 f(x) f^{\prime}(x)+2 g(x) g^{\prime}(x)\right)\left(f^2(-x)+g^2(-x)\right)+\left(f^2(x)+g^2(x)\right)\left(-2 f(-x) f^{\prime}(-x)-2 g(-x) g^{\prime}(-x)\right)$...(3)

Using (1) and (2) in equation (3), we get $F^{\prime}(x)=0$ $\Rightarrow F(x)$ is constant function $\Rightarrow F(0)=1=F(1)$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
